claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 14 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm inquiring about my two children - Alex, who is 14, and Blake, who is 19.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have two children: Alex (age 14) and Blake (age 19).

Before I assess their eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about their circumstances.

**First, do both Alex and Blake currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't both live with me. Alex doesn't live with me, but Blake does.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So Blake lives with you, but Alex doesn't.

**Can you tell me where Alex lives and who Alex lives with?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent - my ex-partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Alex lives with your ex-partner.

Now, let me ask about Blake who lives with you:

**Is Blake in full-time education, apprenticeship, or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake is in full-time education - doing A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good to know, thank you. Blake is doing A-levels full-time.

Now I have a question about Alex:

**Is Alex in full-time education, apprenticeship, or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 14, so yes, they're still in school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that Alex is still in school.

I need to gather a bit more information to complete my assessment. Let me ask about the living arrangements:

**Since Alex lives with your ex-partner, are you still involved in supporting Alex financially, or is your ex-partner the main provider of support?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My ex-partner is the main provider - they're already claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's an important detail.

I now have sufficient information to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules and make my determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I can now make my assessment:

**For Alex:** You are **not eligible** for Child Benefit. Since Alex lives with your ex-partner (not with you), and your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, only one person can get Child Benefit for each child. Your ex-partner is the current claimant.

**For Blake:** You **are eligible** for Child Benefit. Blake lives with you and is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels), which means you can continue to claim Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
In summary:
- **Alex**: Not eligible (ex-partner is already claiming)
- **Blake**: Eligible (lives with you and in full-time education)

You can claim Child Benefit for Blake. If your circumstances change or if you'd like to explore any other options, feel free to contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

reasonings: Alex does not live with you - Alex lives with your ex-partner, who is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim for Alex.

Blake lives with you and is under 20 and in approved full-time education (A-levels). You are responsible for Blake and meet the eligibility criteria. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Blake only. You cannot claim for Alex because your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit and only one person can claim per child. For Blake, you meet the eligibility criteria as Blake lives with you and is in approved education whilst under 20.">
